mission.title: seaweedfs: Question: Recommended way to back up SeaweedFS cluster data (excluding some paths) for disaster recovery?
mission.description: Question: Recommended way to back up SeaweedFS cluster data (excluding some paths) for disaster recovery?. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current seaweedfs deployment
steps.description:
Verify your seaweedfs version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
helm list -n seaweedfs 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working seaweedfs installation.

steps.title: Review seaweedfs configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant seaweedfs configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
kubectl get configmap -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=seaweedfs
```
Hi, I would like to find the *official and recommended* method for backing up a SeaweedFS cluster for disaster recovery (DR), especially when I need to **exclude specific paths** from the backup.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Question: Recommended way to back up SeaweedFS cluster data…
steps.description:
Added a background task that periodically syncs file metadata for open files to the filer, ensuring chunk references are visible to `volume.fsck` even before files are closed.
```yaml
-metadataSyncSec (default: 120)
  Periodically sync file metadata to filer in seconds.
  Set to 0 to disable.
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n seaweedfs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=seaweedfs
kubectl get events -n seaweedfs --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Question: Recommended way to back up SeaweedFS cluster data…" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Added a background task that periodically syncs file metadata for open files to the filer, ensuring chunk references are visible to `volume.fsck` even before files are closed.
codeSnippets:
-metadataSyncSec (default: 120)
  Periodically sync file metadata to filer in seconds.
  Set to 0 to disable.
